intake.risk_posture: Preserve existing /builder behavior strictly; allow new visible UX only on isolated /builder-v2 with explicit product/design stop points.
intake.harness_policy: Product docs and goal packet updates allowed; no AGENTS.md, lint, dependency, or plugin changes without a routed maintenance task and explicit approval.
intake.stop_condition: Stop when the active tranche lands and is verified, a product/design decision is needed, verification fails twice on the same blocker, same-concept expansion crosses protected scope, or the work starts drifting into rushed public-shipping increments rather than healthy local groundwork.

focus_areas.name: New route and feature boundary
focus_areas.evidence: W1 added a lazy BuilderV2Page route in src/App.tsx and kept Builder V2 absent from NAV_ITEMS.
focus_areas.next: Keep /builder-v2 unpromoted until a later product/design decision.

focus_areas.name: Current builder contract reuse
focus_areas.evidence: W1 added useBuilderV2Model as a facade over builderDraftStore, persistence, ownership hydration, search helpers, and team-state assignment/removal helpers; W2 extended it to gear/posse assignment; W3 wired quick-lineup mode through existing builderDraftStore quick-lineup actions.
focus_areas.next: Extend the facade for import/export, transfer parity, DnD, recommendations, and mobile drawer orchestration in queued tranches.

focus_areas.name: Old UX branch flow salvage
focus_areas.evidence: W4/R4 implemented the concept-image mobile overview -> focused builder -> full-screen picker drawer flow on /builder-v2 while keeping the old builder/mobile-ux branch as reference only.
focus_areas.next: Keep old branch evidence available only for future tablet/team polish; do not migrate old branch code into Builder V2.

focus_areas.name: Test and characterization harness
focus_areas.evidence: W1-W4 added focused V2 model/page characterization, including mobile drawer dialog focus, Escape/focus return, reopened target assignment, and mobile quick-lineup focused-slot synchronization.
focus_areas.next: Add focused characterization tests with each later workflow slice.

focus_areas.name: Visual baseline
focus_areas.evidence: W1 added local builder-v2 CSS using sharp panels, compact controls, restrained blue, amber selection state, and D-Zone-style UI tokens.
focus_areas.next: Run deeper visual review/polish in later design tranches.

focus_areas.name: Concept image alignment
focus_areas.evidence: W1 follows Desktop.png anatomy; W3 implemented the quick-lineup functional contract; W4 implemented Mobile.png-style overview/focused/full-screen picker drawer states around that contract.
focus_areas.next: Use concept images again for tablet adaptation, teams overview, and final designer handoff notes.

focus_areas.name: Teams section polish
focus_areas.evidence: User said core builder first and teams shape is undecided.
focus_areas.next: Keep minimal team switching; defer list/cards decision.

focus_areas.name: DnD/import/export/quick-lineup polish
focus_areas.evidence: Current helpers exist but raise larger interaction blast radius.
focus_areas.next: Defer until base draft loop works.

focus_areas.name: Designer handoff readiness
focus_areas.evidence: User affirmed the entire goal packet should lead into handoff to the designer/design model after the V2 core is healthy.
focus_areas.next: Add a final handoff bundle once core V2 interaction slices are implemented and reviewed.

candidates.title: Builder V2 route and bare draft loop shell
candidates.evidence: W1 added src/features/builder-v2 and a lazy /builder-v2 route without nav promotion.

candidates.title: Thin Builder V2 model facade over current store/helpers
candidates.evidence: W1 implemented useBuilderV2Model with current builderDraftStore hydration/autosave and team-state assignment/removal helpers.
candidates.next: Extend the facade in later gear/posse/import tranches.

candidates.title: Minimal V2 picker surface
candidates.evidence: W1 added BuilderV2AwakenerPicker with current search/sort helpers and disabled future category tabs.
candidates.next: C4 broadens picker behavior to wheels, covenants, and posses.

candidates.title: Concept-image-informed V2 shell anatomy
candidates.evidence: W1 implemented the desktop rail/stage/armory/overview shell and mobile single-column collapse, with quick-lineup/drawer polish explicitly kept for later.
candidates.next: Use the concept images again when C5/C6 are active.

candidates.title: Gear and posse assignment
candidates.evidence: W2 added Builder V2 Armory category tabs, actionable W1/W2/Covenant and team Posse targets, click-to-assign/clear flows, same-active-team gear movement, duplicate guards, and focused tests.
candidates.next: Full transfer dialogs, DnD, recommendations, and mobile drawer remain queued in C5/C6/C8 or future polish slices.

candidates.title: Mobile overview/focused/picker drawer flow
candidates.evidence: W4/R4 added a mobile-only Builder V2 layout with overview, focused slot builder, shared picker drawer content, dialog semantics, Escape/search focus/focus return, mobile quick-lineup step focus synchronization, focused tests, and browser smoke.
candidates.next: Tablet adaptation, teams overview shape, transfer/import/export parity, and designer handoff remain queued in C7/C8/C9 or later scoped candidates.

candidates.title: Quick Lineup V2 mode
candidates.evidence: W3 enabled Builder V2 Quick Lineup mode using existing builderDraftStore quick-lineup actions, compact V2 controls, assignment-driven step advance, manual target jump, cancel restore, final posse completion, and focused model/page tests.
candidates.next: C5 mobile overview/focused/full-screen picker drawer flow is the next structurally ready candidate; C7/C8 remain queued.

candidates.title: Teams overview redesign
candidates.evidence: User is undecided between list/cards; core builder first.
candidates.next: Still queued; require product/design decision on list vs cards before deeper team overview work.

candidates.title: Import/export and transfer parity
candidates.evidence: Current page tests pin these workflows; helpers exist but UI blast radius is larger.
candidates.next: Still queued; import/export and transfer parity have larger shared-contract/UI blast radius and should follow core guided flow work.

candidates.title: Designer handoff bundle
candidates.evidence: User clarified the goal packet should lead into handoff to the designer/design model.
candidates.next: Prepare near the end of the local rebuild, after core V2 flow is functional enough that design work is not forced to solve product architecture.

maintenance.title: Builder V2 contract docs
maintenance.next: Consider after first Worker proves a stable boundary.

maintenance.title: Builder visual vocabulary drift guard
maintenance.next: Only if repeated drift appears during Worker/Review.

tasks.title: Map Builder V2 rebuild scope
tasks.expected_output: Evidence map for contracts, UX flow, tests, visual anchors, route integration, and interaction risks.

receipt.summary: Six read-only scouts mapped Builder V2 contracts, old branch UX flow, test coverage, visual anchors, route integration, and core interaction risks.
evidence: Current contracts: reuse builderDraftStore, public-id migrations/persistence, team-state helpers, ownership projection, action factories, import/export helpers, DnD ids, and detail modal store.
Old branch: keep mobile overview/focused/picker/quick-lineup and tablet/desktop flow as reference only; drop old v2 store/persistence/action integration.
Tests: pure helper/store tests carry; current page tests are DOM-coupled and should be translated into V2 outcome tests.
Design: anchor to DESIGN/PRODUCT tokens, D-Zone/Timeline/database patterns, and docs/design screenshots; avoid old builder blue vocabulary.
Routing: add lazy /builder-v2 route in src/App.tsx, avoid nav entry in first slice.
Interaction risks: avoid direct slot mutation; keep helper paths for realm caps, duplicate identity, support transfer, quick-lineup focus, and overlay details.

tasks.title: Approve first Builder V2 Worker slice
tasks.expected_output: One bounded Worker concept scope for C1/C2/C3 with invariants, validation, rollback, and stop conditions.

receipt.summary: Approved first Worker as C1 plus smallest viable C2/C3: an isolated /builder-v2 route and bare awakener draft loop shell.
receipt.concept: Builder V2 route and bare awakener draft loop shell
allowed_files: src/features/builder-v2/*.css only if Tailwind classes become unreadable
invariants: /builder-v2 is lazy-routed but absent from navigation.
V2 uses public ids and existing builderDraftStore; no new persistence/store/migration contract.
Slot assignment/removal goes through current helper/model paths.
Concept images guide layout density and responsive shape without being imported into runtime UI.

tasks.title: Map concept image cues for first Builder V2 shell
tasks.expected_output: First-slice visual/layout cues and deferrals from concept images.

receipt.summary: Visual scout mapped non-negotiable first-slice cues from the concept images.
evidence: Desktop shell should read as left team rail, center active builder, right picker/armory, with team overview below.
Active team area should expose shallow team/posse header, quick lineup placeholder, four slot cards, and selected/editing state.
Mobile concepts should guide future app-like flow, not force full mobile drawer and quick-lineup implementation into the first slice.
Amber should remain state/current selection rather than decorative glow.

tasks.title: Build isolated Builder V2 awakener draft loop shell
tasks.expected_output: Lazy /builder-v2 route, Builder V2 feature folder, thin model facade, concept-informed shell anatomy, four-slot active team UI, minimal awakener picker, assign/remove behavior, and focused tests.

receipt.slice: Builder V2 route and bare awakener draft loop shell
receipt.what_got_simpler: Builder V2 now has an isolated route, feature folder, and typed model facade so the shell can render and mutate draft slot state without importing the current BuilderPage UI.
receipt.behavior_preserved: /builder remained untouched; V2 assignment/removal goes through current team-state helpers, current draft store, current persistence loader/saver, current search helpers, and current identity duplicate handling.
receipt.current_complexity: Current production builder remains unchanged and debt-heavy; V2 first slice is one route component, one model hook, two presentational surfaces, local CSS, and focused tests.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: Later slices can add gear, posse, mobile drawer, teams, and import/export by extending the facade/components instead of editing the current monolith.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root-fix slice: create a new Builder V2 boundary instead of patching the current BuilderPage.
receipt.dependency_review: No dependency changes made or needed.
receipt.harness_update: No AGENTS.md, lint, or plugin harness changes made; goal packet updated only.
validation: npx vitest run src/features/builder-v2/useBuilderV2Model.test.ts src/features/builder-v2/BuilderV2Page.test.tsx (2 files, 10 tests passed)
npx vitest run src/features/builder/useBuilderViewModel.test.ts src/features/builder/builder-persistence.test.ts src/features/builder/builder-ownership-projection.test.ts (3 files, 50 tests passed)
npm run test:integration (7 files, 57 tests passed)
Playwright smoke: http://127.0.0.1:5173/#/builder-v2 desktop 1440x1000 and mobile 390x844 rendered; console output was only React DevTools info
risks: V2 currently shares the existing builder persistence key by design, so visiting /builder-v2 can hydrate/save the same local draft as /builder.
Mobile is a responsive stack only; drawer, app-like focused flows, and quick-lineup are still queued.
Teams overview remains intentionally minimal pending list-vs-cards product direction.
learning_lint_candidates: Consider a Builder V2 contract note after review if the facade boundary holds through the next tranche.

tasks.title: Review W1 Builder V2 route and draft loop shell
tasks.expected_output: Review the W1 diff, receipts, validation, and residual risk; decide whether the tranche needs fixes or can queue the next Builder V2 slice.

receipt.summary: Review found one blocking data-integrity issue, fixed in W1 before tranche close.
findings: V2 initially assigned awakeners through active-team helpers only, so an awakener already used by another non-support team could be duplicated across teams and autosaved into shared builder persistence.
fix: useBuilderV2Model now builds a non-support usedAwakenerByIdentityKey map like V1, respects the current allow-duplicates preference key, and blocks cross-team assignments when V2 has no transfer dialog available.
useBuilderV2Model.test.ts added a regression test proving an awakener already assigned to Team 2 cannot be assigned into Team 1 from V2.
validation: npx vitest run src/features/builder-v2/useBuilderV2Model.test.ts src/features/builder-v2/BuilderV2Page.test.tsx (2 files, 10 tests passed)
npx vitest run src/features/builder/useBuilderViewModel.test.ts src/features/builder/builder-persistence.test.ts src/features/builder/builder-ownership-projection.test.ts (3 files, 50 tests passed)
npm run test:integration (7 files, 57 tests passed)
residual_risk: Full transfer dialog parity remains queued for a later V2 slice.
Untracked screenshot/mockup artifacts remain intentionally outside the product commit unless selected later.

tasks.expected_output: Evidence map for C4 wheel, covenant, and posse contracts, tests, and least-polished usable V2 UI shape.

receipt.summary: Three read-only scouts mapped C4 contracts, pinned tests, and concept-image-aligned UI shape.
evidence: TeamSlot already carries wheels and covenantId, while Team carries posseId.
Existing pure helpers cover wheel and covenant assign, clear, and swap behavior in team-state.ts.
Posse is team-level state handled through active team updates; V1 transfer behavior remains a larger deferred parity concern.
V1 tests pin same-team wheel movement, cross-team transfer requests, covenant assignment/swap, and posse transfer behavior.
C4 UI should make W1/W2/Covenant strip targets real buttons, make the header Posse target actionable, and activate Armory tabs for Awakeners, Wheels, Covenants, and Posses.
Mobile drawer, DnD, and full transfer dialogs should remain deferred for this slice.

receipt.current_complexity: Builder V2 now has one model hook, one armory surface with four tabs, one slot card surface with actionable loadout targets, and focused tests for the assignment loop.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: Later slices can add transfer dialogs, DnD, recommendations, quick-lineup, and mobile drawer behavior without first inventing core gear/posse state flow.

tasks.title: Review W2 Builder V2 gear and posse assignment flow
tasks.expected_output: Review W2 diff, receipts, validation, residual risk, and scope boundaries.

receipt.summary: Review found two low-severity no-op repeat-click issues, both fixed before tranche close.
findings: Repeatedly clicking an already assigned wheel on its selected socket showed a false failure message.
Repeatedly clicking an already assigned covenant on its selected slot showed a false failure message.
fix: useBuilderV2Model now treats no-op same-wheel and same-covenant assignment as quiet state preservation.
useBuilderV2Model.test.ts added a regression test for repeated wheel and covenant assignments.

receipt.summary: Three read-only scouts mapped mobile drawer/focused flow, quick-lineup contracts, and queued candidate ordering after W2.
evidence: Mobile.png defines C5 as three mobile states: Team Overview, Active Character Builder, and Picker as a full-screen drawer, not the current stacked armory.
builder/mobile-ux modeled overview/focused/picker/quick-lineup flow, but should remain flow evidence only because its store/persistence and drawer semantics are outdated.
quick-lineup.ts and builderDraftStore already expose reusable quick-lineup session, focus, advance, back, cancel, finish, and jump behavior.
V2 has assignment actions for awakeners, wheels, covenants, and posses after W2, so C6 is unlocked without touching shared store/helper behavior.
C7 remains product/design blocked by list-vs-cards uncertainty; C8 remains broader parity work around import/export and transfer dialogs.
receipt.recommendation: C6 is structurally safer before C5 because it proves guided focus/picker synchronization before mobile drawer focus management.

receipt.summary: Approved C6 as the next bounded Builder V2 Worker slice, ahead of C5 mobile drawer work.
receipt.decision: Activate C6: Quick Lineup V2 mode. Do not activate C5 yet.
in_scope: Enable the existing disabled Quick Team Lineup button on /builder-v2.
Expose quickLineupSession and start/advance/back/skip/finish/cancel actions through useBuilderV2Model by consuming existing builderDraftStore actions.
Synchronize quick-lineup focus into V2 pickerTab, activeSelection, and activeTeamTarget, including the posse step.
Add compact visible quick-lineup controls/status in the active team surface.
After successful V2 assignments, advance to the next quick-lineup step using existing store/helper semantics.
Support cancel restoring the original active team state and finish ending quick-lineup without changing /builder.
Keep controls keyboard-accessible with real buttons and stable labels.
deferred: C5 mobile overview/focused/full-screen picker drawer and focus trap.
Transfer dialogs and cross-team move confirmations.
Any shared store/helper behavior changes unless scope expansion is approved.
validation: Browser smoke /builder-v2 at desktop and mobile widths: start quick lineup, assign awakener, advance/back, cancel restores state.

tasks.title: Build Builder V2 Quick Lineup mode
tasks.expected_output: Enable Builder V2 Quick Lineup mode through the V2 model/page/components/tests, using existing builderDraftStore quick-lineup actions and preserving all shared builder behavior.

receipt.what_got_simpler: Builder V2 now exposes a single guided quick-lineup path through the existing V2 model facade instead of keeping the Quick Team Lineup entry point as inert UI.
receipt.behavior_preserved: /builder files and shared builderDraftStore/quick-lineup helpers were not edited; V2 consumes existing quick-lineup store actions and keeps W1/W2 duplicate and assignment guards intact.
receipt.current_complexity: Builder V2 has a model-owned quickLineupSession view, compact page controls, and existing slot/picker surfaces that synchronize through activeSelection/pickerTab.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: C5 mobile drawer/focused layout can consume a proven guided focus contract instead of inventing separate mobile selection semantics.
data_access_render_invariants: Quick-lineup uses public-id Team/TeamSlot contracts from the current builder store.
Starting quick-lineup clears the active team via existing builderDraftStore behavior and cancel restores the original team.
Successful V2 assignment actions advance quick-lineup without bypassing existing team-state helper validation.
During quick-lineup, destructive clear/remove controls are hidden from V2 slot cards to avoid conflicting with guided focus.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Bounded root-fix slice inside the Builder V2 facade/UI boundary; no old BuilderPage edits and no shared store/helper changes.
receipt.dependency_review: No dependency changes made or needed.
receipt.characterization: Added V2 model/page tests for start, assignment advance, back/next, cancel restore, manual target jump, and final posse completion; existing V1 builder suite stayed green.
receipt.harness_update: No AGENTS.md, lint, dependency, or plugin harness changes made; goal packet updated only.

tasks.expected_output: Read-only evidence for the C5 mobile overview/focused/full-screen picker drawer implementation seam and focused failing tests.

evidence: Current Builder V2 data flow already exposes pickerTab, activeSelection, activeTeamTarget, quickLineupSession, assignment actions, and quick-lineup controls through useBuilderV2Model.
Current mobile behavior is CSS stacking only; it does not provide concept-image overview/focused/picker app states, dialog semantics, Escape handling, or focus return.

The C5 implementation seam should keep model logic in useBuilderV2Model, keep desktop behavior stable, extract reusable picker content from the current armory aside, and add mobile-only orchestration under src/features/builder-v2/**.
The smallest useful tests should assert mobile picker dialog naming, correct picker tab/search focus for slot and wheel targets, Escape close, and focus return to the invoking control.
Out of scope remains shared store/helper changes, current /builder edits, import/export, DnD, transfer dialogs, recommendations, and deep teams redesign.

tasks.title: Build C5 mobile overview and picker drawer flow
tasks.expected_output: Mobile overview/focused/full-screen picker drawer flow with shared picker content, dialog focus management, TDD coverage, and preserved desktop Builder V2 behavior.

receipt.slice: C5 mobile overview/focused/full-screen picker drawer flow
behavior_added: Mobile /builder-v2 now branches into an app-like overview, focused slot builder, and full-screen picker drawer at <= 768px.
Desktop Builder V2 keeps the existing rail/workbench/armory shell and reuses extracted picker content.
Mobile slot, wheel, covenant, and posse targets open a named dialog with the correct picker tab and search focus.
Escape closes the mobile picker and returns focus to the invoking control; reopened same-target pickers preserve their assignment target.
Mobile quick-lineup derives the visible focused slot from quickLineupSession.currentStep so assignment advance and Back/Next stay aligned with the guided target.
behavior_preserved: /builder files, shared builderDraftStore, persistence/migrations, generated data, dependencies, route glue, and global CSS were not edited.
Builder V2 still uses the existing useBuilderV2Model assignment and quick-lineup store semantics rather than introducing a mobile-specific model.
Untracked docs/design and smoke screenshot artifacts were left unstaged/excluded from W4 packaging.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Bounded root-fix inside the isolated Builder V2 UI orchestration boundary: mobile focus state now derives from the model-owned quick-lineup step instead of duplicating guided selection semantics.
receipt.dependency_review: No dependency changes made or needed.
characterization: Observed red run before implementation: BuilderV2Page mobile tests failed because overview/dialog/focus behavior did not exist.
Observed red run during browser-found edge case: reopened same mobile target after Escape assigned to the wrong slot until the target selection guard was added.
Observed red run from reviewer finding: quick-lineup Step 5 showed Slot 2 in model text while the focused panel still showed Slot 1/Goliath until derived quick-lineup focus landed.

Added page tests for mobile overview, focused slot, drawer search focus, wheel tab selection, Escape focus return, reopened same-target assignment, and quick-lineup focused-slot sync through assignment plus Back/Next.

npm test -- --run src/features/builder (33 files, 263 tests passed)
npm run test:integration (7 files, 57 tests passed)
npm run build (passed; existing Vite chunk-size and plugin timing warnings only)

Browser smoke: /builder-v2 desktop 1440x1000 assigned Goliath and Merciful Nurturing with no dialog; mobile 390x844 covered overview, drawer search focus, Escape focus return, reopened Slot 2 assignment, quick-lineup Step 2, and Slot 1 -> Slot 2 quick-lineup focus sync with Back/Next.
receipt.review: R4 initially found a high quick-lineup focus drift and medium untracked-artifact packaging note; the focus drift was fixed and the final reviewer verdict was complete with no findings.
risks: Successful picker assignment closes without explicit focus restoration because the visible target panel can change after assignment.
Final quick-lineup completion/cancel may fall back to the last manual focus slot; reviewer did not consider this blocking for C5.
Teams overview redesign, tablet-specific layout, transfer dialogs, DnD, recommendations, and import/export parity remain queued.

tasks.expected_output: Review W4 diff, receipts, validation, mobile drawer semantics, quick-lineup focus sync, protected scope, and packaging boundaries before C5 can be marked implemented.

receipt.summary: Review passed after one required fix.
initial_findings: High: mobile quick-lineup focus could drift from the active quick-lineup target after advancing from Slot 1 covenant to Slot 2 awakener.
Medium: untracked docs/design and smoke screenshots must stay excluded from W4 packaging unless explicitly selected.
fixes_reviewed: BuilderV2MobileLayout now derives focusedSlotId from quickLineupSession.currentStep while quick-lineup is active and uses manual focus only for normal mobile browsing.
BuilderV2Page.test.tsx covers Slot 1 completion, automatic Slot 2 focus, and Back/Next mobile focus synchronization.
Untracked docs/design and screenshot artifacts are explicitly excluded from staging/commit for W4.

Browser targeted smoke for mobile quick-lineup Slot 1 -> Slot 2 focus sync with Back/Next
residual_risk: Successful picker assignment closes without explicit focus restoration because the visible panel may change after assignment.
Final quick-lineup completion/cancel may fall back to the last manual focus slot; not blocking for C5.
